project.name: Turkmen Quran Translation Perso-Arabic Transliteration
project.purpose: Maintain cleaned Turkmen Latin Quran translation data and generate Afghan Turkmen Perso-Arabic transliteration artifacts.

agent_operations.scope_decision: Implemented the useful control layer from the max architecture, not all 144 speculative files.

orthography.principle: Strict phonetic transliteration of Turkmen, with conventional Arabic spellings for Islamic and Arabic-origin terms.
important_rules: Use explicit vowel carriers for word-initial vowels: ئە, ئی, ئو, ئۆ, ئۇ, ئۈ.
Use ھ for Turkmen h, ک for k, ڭ for ň, ژ for ž, چ for ç.
Do not use Arabic API/LLM calls; transliteration is deterministic and local.
Preserve Turkmen words; do not translate into Arabic or Persian.
Use conventional spellings for terms such as الله, رحمن, رحیم, قرآن, نماز, عبادت.

qa_status.source_issue_policy: Rows with embedded verse-number fragments are corrected through source_corrections.json using v2 candidates and deterministic split fixes, then rechecked.
qa_status.known_blocker: No hard blockers remain after applying 57 source corrections. Broad suspect rows remain review hints, not release blockers.

decisions.decision: Use Turkmen.json as better master than earlier CSVs.
decisions.reason: Earlier CSV had REVIEW_NEEDED and garbled rows; Turkmen.json had complete 6236-row structure.
Düşündiriş means explanation/commentary, not verse text.
A deterministic local transliteration pipeline is reproducible, cheaper, and avoids non-deterministic output.

decisions.decision: Quarantine corrupted source rows instead of auto-inventing corrections.
decisions.reason: Sacred text publication requires source authority; column-interleaved PDF rows cannot be safely guessed.

decisions.decision: Implement a compact agent-operations layer instead of scaffolding all 144 proposed architecture files.
decisions.reason: The local pipeline benefits from memory, rules, success criteria, failure patterns, fix strategies, and audit trail without unnecessary unused modules.

decisions.decision: Add source correction overlay before source QA.
decisions.reason: The pipeline can now apply authoritative fixes from work/source_corrections.json and automatically graduate corrected ayahs on the next run.

decisions.decision: Generate PDF-derived correction candidates as review aids only.
decisions.reason: Column-aware extraction found candidates for 45 of 57 blockers, but samples show some are partial or wrong-page; they must not be auto-applied without review.

next_actions: Spot-review the 57 applied source corrections, especially v2-derived rows where letters may be dropped.
Review broad source-quality hints in Turkmen_source_quality_report.csv.
Package the release-candidate JSON/CSV, reports, Brain.json, and README.
Optionally build SQLite export for app/search use.
maintenance_rule: Update this Brain.json after every significant source, pipeline, QA, or output change.
